$comment: s-dna-port-surface-parity → golden surface lock. THE public surface manifest of the Python SDK: each port lists the members of its real typing.Protocol; ManifestInstance/Kernel list the blessed vs deprecated read surface plus the EXACT public_surface. Adding, removing or renaming any tracked member without editing this file turns the suite red — public-surface changes are conscious decisions, never drift. Executors: packages/sdk-py/tests/test_port_surface_golden.py (introspects the real typing.Protocol members), test_blessed_query_surface.py and test_hook_names.py. History: this was a shared Py↔TS manifest with a {py, ts} pair per member; the TypeScript SDK was frozen (tag sdk-ts-final) and the `ts` half was dropped — the Python surface lock, which is what it always really enforced, stays.

SourcePort.doc: WHERE — load documents from storage. Py: dna/kernel/protocols.py · TS: src/kernel/protocols.ts

members.note: Py lists close in SOURCE_PORT_CORE_MEMBERS (boot gate); TS declares it optional — the kernel treats a missing close as a no-op. FS adapters implement a documented no-op; the SQL adapters end what they own (Py SqlAlchemySource disposes its engine; TS raw PostgresSource closes its pool).
L1 granular read, capability-mediated on both sides (SourceCapabilities.granular_list / granularList).

WritableSourcePort.doc: SourcePort + write/versioning half. OWN members only (inherited SourcePort members are tracked above).

members.note: PG TS is a documented single-step no-op (no draft state); the member exists so Draftable-style flows type-check.

ReaderPort.doc: Reads a bundle and produces a raw dict.

WriterPort.doc: Writes a raw dict back to a bundle. Inverse of ReaderPort.

KindPresentation.doc: Optional presentation/UX capability of a Kind (s-dna-kindport-descriptor-schema). Py: typing-only capability Protocol (NOT runtime_checkable, NOT part of KindPort — the H1 isinstance gate must never require these; the is_runtime_artifact precedent). TS: interface that KindPort extends (native optional members). Every member is optional at runtime; KindBase carries the attribute defaults; consumers use typed access with a default (getattr(kp, name, None) / kp.member?.).

ToolPort.doc: An invocable tool exposed to agents + DNA discovery metadata (s-dna-tool-decorator; TS twin landed with s-dna-port-surface-parity).

ExtensionHost.doc: The registration-time surface the Kernel offers to Extension.register() (s-dna-extension-host-contract).

Extension.doc: Registers kinds, readers, and writers on the Kernel.

TemplateProvider.doc: Optional Extension capability — ships scaffold file trees. Py-only Protocol; TS folds the member into Extension.templates?.

RecordSearchProvider.doc: Two-planes F2 — semantic search over record docs, registered on the kernel at app boot.

EmbeddingPort.doc: rec-embedding-port — text→dense-vector, sibling to RecordSearchProvider. Fake floor is zero-dep + bit-exact Py↔TS; ONNX all-MiniLM-L6-v2 is an opt-in extra. Py: dna/kernel/protocols.py · TS: src/kernel/protocols.ts

SourceCapabilities.doc: Typed declaration of what a source adapter supports (s-sourceport-contract-cleanup). Members are dataclass fields (Py) / interface keys (TS).

CapabilityProtocols.doc: Exported optional-capability Protocols (Py capabilities.py) / interfaces (TS capabilities.ts). Members here are the PROTOCOL NAMES, not method names.

blessed_query_surface.$comment: s-blessed-query-surface — THE public read/query surface of the SDK, pre-1.0. `blessed` members are the ONE documented way to read manifest data (docs/ + README + examples teach ONLY these). `deprecated` members still work but warn (DeprecationWarning) — always naming the exact replacement — and are removed in 1.0. `public_surface` is the EXACT set of public members on an instantiated ManifestInstance: adding/removing/renaming ANY public MI member without editing this fixture turns the suite red. Executor: packages/sdk-py/tests/test_blessed_query_surface.py.

blessed.note: canonical in-memory read — filter/find by d.kind / d.name; what QUICK-START, READMEs and examples teach

deprecated.replacement: mi.documents (filter by d.kind) or kernel.query(scope, kind)
mi.documents (find by d.kind + d.name); Py also kernel.get_document(scope, kind, name), TS kernel.query with a filter

blessed.note: entry point — loads/returns the scope's ManifestInstance
indexed / record-plane read — push-down to the source, raw rows

hook_names.$comment: s-dna-typed-hook-names — the HookRegistry hook-name vocabulary: HookName Literal + KNOWN_HOOK_NAMES (dna/kernel/hooks.py). Executor: packages/sdk-py/tests/test_hook_names.py — vocabulary drift turns the suite red. These names are wire vocabulary, not API casing.

collaborator-ports.justification: Kernel INTERNALS, not public contract: these are the narrow mediator slices from the kernel-decomposition epic (e-kernel-decomposition). They are free to change shape without a surface review, so they are deliberately NOT tracked as a port.
